title: freirea pensylvanica
intro: Freirea pensylvanica, also known as the Philippine mulberry, is a plant species native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering potential health benefits such as reduced pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
